source.title: Population coding of predator imminence in the hypothalamus.

confidence_rationale: Recent Anderson-lab paper showing VMHdm population dynamics encode predator imminence as a continuous variable, with anti-correlated subpopulations encoding threat-presence vs threat-absence. The ramp/decay dynamics of these populations directly characterise what an upstream-of-PAG harm signal looks like in vivo, and the existence of an anti-correlated 'safety' subpopulation is direct evidence that the harm vs no-harm decision is computed in VMHdm population dynamics rather than purely downstream. Confidence high because the imaging is large-scale GCaMP across many cells and the analysis cleanly separates encoding subpopulations.
failure_signatures: Anti-correlated 'threat-on' and 'threat-off' subpopulations in VMHdm -- the hypothalamus itself implements a winner-take-all arbitration before broadcasting downstream.
Continuous population coding of imminence -- threat is not binary at the source; thresholding happens downstream (consistent with MECH-279).

mapping.source_claim_statement: Calcium imaging of VMHdm during predator exposure reveals population dynamics encoding predator imminence as a continuous variable. Two anti-correlated subpopulations encode threat-presence vs threat-absence, with their relative activation tracking imminence. The encoding is at the population level, not at single-cell level.
mapping.ree_translation: For V3, this paper supports two specific architectural choices: (1) z_harm should be a continuous-valued signal (population code) at the upstream level, with threshold conversion happening at downstream arbiters (consistent with MECH-279 PAG gate); (2) z_safety should be modeled as the anti-correlated complement of z_harm, with their relative activation determining defensive engagement. Currently V3 has only z_harm; the explicit anti-correlated 'safety' channel is a missing degree of freedom.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The anti-correlated subpopulations are within VMHdm; whether the same anti-correlation structure exists in the broader harm-coding network (PBN, CeA, BNST) is not addressed. The paper studies acute predator presentation; sustained-threat dynamics relevant to V3-EXQ-471 lock-in are not characterised.
mapping.source_context: Two-photon calcium imaging in VMHdm of mice during exposure to live and inanimate predator stimuli, with population-level analyses (PCA, decoding).

confidence_components.notes: Recent Neuron paper from Anderson lab. Mapping fidelity high for population coding architecture; the anti-correlated safety subpopulation is a novel finding with direct V3 implications.
